title: case-file eligibility: status-safety must apply at row level, not just summary
content: ADR 0001 fixed the unsafe `status !== "Partial" → qualifying/success` assumption only in the summary badge; the identical bug survived per-row in CriteriaTable.toneFor(), so an unknown/absent AI-scored status renders a GREEN 'met' badge while summarizeCriteria correctly excludes it — table and summary disagree on a legal eligibility surface. Lesson: when centralizing a data-safety rule (ADR 0001), audit ALL render sites that consume the same status field, not just the one flagged. Also: CriteriaTable hardcoded denominator '8' and threshold '3' instead of criteria.length / QUALIFYING_THRESHOLD. format.ts is unused and sound — not a defect source. Resolved in ADR 0002 (7 story points).

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 18:43 UTC
Immigration Concierge — case-file data layer is the key tech-debt seam
content: In the immigration-concierge codebase (O-1A visa petition product), the case-file UI (CriteriaTable, CaseFileDashboard) imports hardcoded sample data (the 'Dr. Krishnan' fixture) directly from data.ts at module level. There is no data-source boundary, so the dashboard can only render one static case — this is the single biggest blocker to the multi-case evidence-vault epics (E2/E4 in docs/BACKLOG.md). Any future work here must preserve the ADR 0002 status-safety invariant: criteria data must flow through summarizeCriteria/statusTone so unscored rows never render as 'met'/green. Secondary recurring observation: src/lib/format.ts ships unused.

Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 18:53 UTC
BL-001 case-file data-coupling is the gating blocker for multi-case epics
content: Confirmed by evidence: src/features/case-file imports the single-case fixture from data.ts at module scope in 4 places (CaseFileDashboard.tsx caseFacts, CriteriaTable.tsx criteria, SidePanels.tsx outstandingTasks+petitionExcerpt, criteria.test.ts criteria). This hardcoded 'Dr. Krishnan / Case O1-241' coupling is the gating blocker for the Week 5 attorney review UI and evidence-vault epics, which require per-case data. Recommended remediation: a getCaseFile(caseId) loader/provider boundary, keeping data.ts as the seed fixture, and preserving the ADR 0002 invariant (criteria must flow through summarizeCriteria/statusTone so unscored rows never render green). Presented for human triage as BL-001; not yet accepted.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 18:54 UTC
Code review verdict — ADR 0002 criteria row tone (commit 207626e): APPROVE
content: Reviewed the case-file criteria row-tone change (commit 207626e, ADR 0002). New statusTone(status: unknown): BadgeTone helper runtime-guards AI-sourced status: Partial→warning, Met|Strong→success, else→neutral — so an unscored/unknown criterion never renders green and the per-row badge agrees with summarizeCriteria. Hardcoded 8/3 replaced by criteria.length / QUALIFYING_THRESHOLD. No critical/security/correctness issues. Only Low note: criteria.test.ts re-derives expected tone via the same Partial-vs-not formula it tests (prefer literal per-status assertions). Verified empirically: tsx --test 8/8 pass, tsc --noEmit clean. ADR 0002 invariant (unknown != success) preserved. Verdict APPROVE. Adjacent BL-001 (data.ts single-case fixture coupling) is a separate untriaged candidate, out of scope.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 18:57 UTC
Verify release event repo matches wired codebase before syncing docs
content: A release.published event can be routed to a Docs Steward whose codebase connector points at a DIFFERENT repo than the one named in the event payload. On 2026-05-27, an 'ai-bookkeeper' v0.3.0 release (ledger/currency features) arrived while this steward was wired to 'Immigration Paperwork' (Meridian, O-1 visa app, tags v0.2.0/v0.2.1, no financial code). Always cross-check payload.repo against CODEBASE_PROJECT_NAME/ROOT_PATH and confirm the payload's changes actually exist in the codebase (git log/grep/tags) before editing any docs. If they don't, do NOT fabricate entries — report the mismatch and flag for routing review.
